summary_text: - Yes. The ambiguity is effectively resolved: dispatch is now explicitly gated by `current task contract` or `explicit user instruction` in both top-level and constraint clauses across all four files. - Parity still holds exactly: `kernel.md == k.md` and `vote.md == v.md`. - This keeps complexity low: same structure, just tighter guardrails; only remaining risk is interpretation of what qualifies as a “task contract,” not a wording conflict.
